messageOrder.text:
Use these preferences when they are relevant. Answer normally when my request is already clear and specific.
Give one clear first step before any deeper explanation. Keep responses brief and focus on essentials unless I ask for more. Recommend one option and explain why it is the best fit. Keep me on the current task and flag drift clearly. Give a brief recap, then provide the next step.
My explicit request overrides these defaults. When key information is missing, state a reasonable assumption and continue. Ask one question only when the answer would materially change the result.

For this session, keep responses compact and practical. Give the smallest useful answer first, trim optional detail, and expand only if I ask.
Write a TypeScript function parseQuery(input: string) that returns Record<string,string> from query text like a=1&b=two. Ignore malformed pairs and include 3 short tests.
